provisions.title: Title and commencement
provisions.content: These By-laws may be cited as the Songea Town Council ( Roads ) By-laws 2001 and shall come into operation on the date of publication in the Government Gazzette.

provisions.title: Definition
provisions.content: In these By-laws— " Authorized Officer " means any person or persons authorized to act for the Council for the purpose of these By-laws. " Council " means Songea Town Council. " Director " means the person for the time being holding the position or acting in the capacity of Town Director of the council and includes his deputy. " Roads " includes any main road, street, any cause way of at the side of any street or a street constructed or set a part for the use of accom­modation of foot passers and includes the verges of any carriageway, sanitary lane, thoroughfare or space to which a roadway passes. " Rubbish " include any kind of refuse, broken nails or glass, broken crock­ery or bottles, stones, hay straw, fins or other containers, dead ani­mals orange peel, banana skin, manure, broken boards of vehicles and waste of dirty water and any other such materials of the same type which may seem to cause injuries to the road users. " Vehicle " includes any motor vehicle, cart, wagon or any other ve­hicle whatsoever whether self—propelled or otherwise.

(1) No person, shall without the written permission of the Direc­tor make the alongside of the road as a parking place or as a business place or pack the vehicle to unauthorized area. (2) No person shall without the written permission of the Town Di­rector pack and or unload any cases, crates, furniture, goods, materials or merchandise in or upon any road, street, foot path, public space or thoroughfare. (3) Any person who contravenes or fails to comply with any of the provisions in this, shall be guilty of an offence and shall be liable on conviction to a fine not exceeding fifty thousand shillings or to imprison­ment for a term not exceeding three months or both such fine and impris­onment.

provisions.title: Penalty
provisions.content: (1) No person shall allow any goods, cases, crates, timber mer­chandise or other particles to remain on any road. (2) No person shall deposit or cause to be deposited any rubbish alongside the road unless he shall first obtain written permission of the council under the hand of the Director and then in accordance with the terms and conditions of such permission.

provisions.title: Miscelaneous nomisance
provisions.content: No person shall: (a) Commit any nuisance in any road, place or public resort. (b) Wilfully deface or remove any milestone or board or any public notice or posting bill from building or place where such notice or bill have been lawfully affixed. (c) Create any disturbance in any road or place of public resort as to amount to an annoyance to the residents or passers. (d) Split upon any footpath, public place thoroughfare reserved for the use of foot passers or in any public service vehicle or in any building to which the public have access. (e) Loiter on any traffic island constructed in any roadway or thor­oughfare or in any way interfere with or obstruct pedestrial traf­fic.

provisions.title: No digging a trench unless permitted
provisions.content: (1) No person shall dig or cause to be dug any excavation, pit, trench, hole or hump for any purpose whatsoever in or close to any road, street, foot path, thoroughfare or public space unless he shall first obtain the written permission of the council under the hand of the Director and only in accordance with the terms and conditions attached to such per­mit. (2) No person, having obtained the permission of the Council ex­ecuted the works referred to in paragraph (1) of this By-law shall leave any hole excavation, trench or other dangerous formation, in or near any road, street, footpath, thoroughfare, or public space without fencing or enclosing the same and keeping a light burning upon such enclosure from sunset to sunrise.

No person shall place, throw down, drop or otherwise deposit in, into or on any road, street, footpath, public space or vacant land in such circumstances as: (a) To be likely to cause injury to pedestrians, walkers, animals or damage to property or (b) To contributed to or tend to lead to the defacement by rubbish unless the depositing and leaving of such things is authorized under these By-laws or is done with the consent of the owner, occupier of other person or authority having control of the place in or into which that things is deposited.

No person shall construct any fence alongside any road, street, footpath, thoroughfare or public space or place open to the public, barbed wire or other material likely to cause injury or damage to any person or to the property of such person.

No person shall; (a) Willfully obstruct, or cause to be done any injury or damage to any road, street, footpath or other public space. (b) Willfully or negligently damage any bridge, wall, building, fence or erection maintainable by the council, or any tree, shrub or vegetable of any kind or upon any road. (c) Break, injure remove or displace any tools, trestles, bars, stones, material, barriers, lamps or other article whatsoever belonging to the Town Council on any street, footpath, road or open space or used thereon under the authority. (d) Break up the surface of any road, street or footpath without hav­ing previously obtained the written consent of the council under the hand of the Town Director and then only in accordance with such conditions as may be specified in such, consent or. (e) Let up elect any structure including any machine, pump, hoard­ing post, KIOSK, stand, pitch or other object of such nature as to be capable of causing any obstruction in any road or street other­wise than in pursuance of any statutory authority, and where any such structure has been set up or erected without permission, The Town Director may serve upon the person having control or possession of the structure with-in such time as may be specified in the notice.

(1) Any person intending to construct footbridge or to alter or reconstruct an existing footbridge over an open drain in any Public Street or road shall make application to the Town Director for permission to carry out the work and shall lodge with the Director a plan in duplicate, on which shall be specified the intended position, the dimensions and the materials proposed to be used in constructing or altering the said footbridge. (2) The provisions of any By-laws made by the Council with respect to streets and building shall be deemed to apply in every respect to the construction and alteration of existing footbridges over open drains in any public street or roads within the Town.

(1) No person shall in alongside any road or street in any public or in any vacant land erect, fix, place or use or permit to be used, any building, hoarding framework, structure or device for the purpose wholly or in part of advertisement without first obtaining the written consent of the council under the hand of the Director , and then only in accordance with such conditions as the council may prescribe. (2) No person shall, for the purpose of advertising throw down and leave any bill, placard or other paper in or upon any road, street footpath or public place. (3) The council shall remove without notice and dispose of at their discretion, any hoarding, signboard, advertising device or similar struc­ture or any bill, sign, notice, or advertisement placed or posted upon any bridge, tree, fence, pole, post or other structure or election situated on or in view of any street within the Township without the permission of the Council or otherwise than in strict accordance with any terms or condi­tions attached to such permission. No liability shall attach to the Coun­cil in respect of their exercise of the power confined upon them by these By-laws.

(1) Any person who fails to comply, with the requirements of any notice served under these By-laws, within the time specified in such notice, shall be guilty of an offence. (2) Where under these By-laws, the council is empowered to serve a notice upon any person requiring the demolition, removal, construction, restructure, alteration or repair of any structure or fitting, the council may, itself enter upon the premises and carry out such demolition, removal, contraction, alterations or repairs, and in addition to any other proceedings that may be taken, the Council may act in accordance with the terms of such notice and may recover all costs and expenses incurred by from the person who has failed to comply with such requirements.

Any person who contravenes or fail to comply with any of the provisions of these By-laws shall be guilty of any offence and shall be liable, on conviction to a fine not exceeding fifty thousand shillings or imprisonment for a term not exceeding three months or both such fine and imprisonment.
